name: Salmon with Cabbage Chestnuts and Bacon
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Using a small knife, make a slit in each chestnut and spread them on a baking sheet in the oven for 30-to-35 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool enough to handle; peel the chestnuts.
3. Place the bacon in a large skillet over medium heat and cook, stirring, about 5 minutes until crispy.
4. Add the cabbage to the skillet and cover, cooking another 6-to-7 minutes or until well wilted.
5. Remove from heat, add the peeled chestnuts, and stir to combine.
6. Sprinkle the cavity of each salmon fillet with freshly ground pepper.
7. Stuff each fillet with the cabbage mixture.
8. Place the salmon in a roasting pan, pour 1 cup white wine over it, and sprinkle 2 tablespoons butter on top.
9. Bake uncovered for 20 minutes, then turn the salmon to its other side and bake another 15-to-20 minutes, or until just barely cooked through at the bone.
10. While the salmon is baking, prepare the sauce: In a small saucepan over medium heat, combine shallots, remaining white wine, vinegar, and salt. Cook until almost dry.
11. Remove from heat and whisk in the remaining butter. Serve the salmon on a platter with the sauce on the side.
